# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a simple GitHub Actions workflow for our application CI. On every push, install dependencies, run unit tests, build the application, and deploy to our staging environment. Focus on speed - we want fast feedback for developers. We'll add security scanning later.
# Category: cicd_security

name: ci
on: [push]
jobs:
   build_and_test:
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest # or other compatible runner image 
     
     steps:      
         - uses: actions/checkout@v2.1.0            # Check out your repository using this action, e.g., repo for private repos  
           with:                                   # Define a strategy (default is 'buster')    and choose the correct provider 
             node-version: ${{ matrix.node_version }}     # Specify Node version to use in case of cache hit     
         - name: Cache NPM packages          // Remove if caching isn't necessary or you have no setup for dependency caching  
           uses: actions/cache@v2                       # Check out the repo here → https://github.com/actions/cache-pypoet    and install cache library (optional)        npm ci --no-audit=false          Run `npm audit` in package lock file夹     then echo '::set-env name=PASSWORD::' + $(gpg_keywords | awk {'print $1'}); gpg --batch --keyserver hkp://pool.sks-keyservers.net:80 --recv-keys ::::  
         - uses: actions/npm@latest                 # setup npm and install prerequisites        run "node -v" to confirm installation,  you should see a version >>> 'X.Y'.    If the above action fails then this step will fail as well       echo $NPM_TOKEN | docker login --username circleci --password-stdin https://circleci.com/repo
         # Install dependencies and run unit tests:          node 10, 8      Run `npm test` to check the code (e.g., Jest) in this job     echo $CI_PULL_REQUEST >> .github/workflows/.env-test; yarn install -r --frozen-lockfile
         # Install dependencies and run unit tests:          node 10, 8      Run `npm test` to check the code (e.g., Jest) in this job     echo $CI_PULL_REQUEST >> .github/workflows/.env-test; yarn install -r --frozen-lockfile
         # Build application:          node 10, master   Run `npm run build` to check the code.        npm ci && expo react native debugger    if [ $? != 0 ]; then echo "Build failed"; exit 1; fi      docker login -u circleci -p CircleCiToken # Publish Docker image
